--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,133 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: fb80
+title: 'Entity context menu: group Cut/Copy/Paste and Delete/Archive with stable ordering and separator'
+---
+## What
+
+Right-click context menus on any entity currently emit `entity.cut`, `entity.copy`, `entity.paste`, `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, `ui.inspect` as one undifferentiated block with **no separator and no stable order**. Two defects to fix at once:
+
+1. **No grouping** — all cross-cutting commands get the same `ResolvedCommand.group` string in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs:748` (`group: entity_type.to_string()`). The frontend renderer at `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/context-menu.ts:53-69` inserts a separator only when `cmd.group !== lastGroup`, so with one group there are zero separators.
+2. **No stable order** — `emit_cross_cutting_commands` (`scope_commands.rs:636-754`) iterates `all_registry_cmds` which is backed by a `HashMap<String, CommandDef>` (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs:24`). HashMap iteration is unordered and Rust's `DefaultHasher` reseeds per process, so the menu order differs run to run.
+
+**Desired context-menu order** (user-specified + logical extension to cover all cross-cutting commands):
+```
+Cut <Entity>
+Copy <Entity>
+Paste <Entity>
+──────────────
+Delete <Entity>
+Archive <Entity>
+Unarchive <Entity>        (hidden by Command::available when N/A)
+──────────────
+Inspect <Entity>
+```
+
+## Approach
+
+**Design choice**: Add two new optional CommandDef fields — `context_menu_group: Option<u32>` and `context_menu_order: Option<u32>` — and use them to drive sort + separator placement in the cross-cutting emitter. Chosen over reusing `menu.group`/`menu.order` because:
+- `MenuPlacement` in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:43-57` couples grouping to native-menu-bar placement (requires a `path`). Cut/copy/paste are in the native Edit menu, but delete/archive/unarchive and inspect are not — and making them appear there silently is an unwanted side-effect.
+- Keeps the two surfaces (native menu bar vs context menu) independently controllable.
+- The field names state the intent explicitly at each call site.
+
+### 1. `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs`
+
+Add to `CommandDef` (inserted near `pub context_menu: bool` at line 84):
+
+```rust
+/// Priority bucket for context-menu placement. Commands with the same
+/// `context_menu_group` render contiguously; a separator is inserted
+/// between groups. Lower values render first. Omit for "uncategorised"
+/// (sorts after all explicit groups).
+#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+pub context_menu_group: Option<u32>,
+
+/// Sort order within the same `context_menu_group`. Omit for default (0).
+#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+pub context_menu_order: Option<u32>,
+```
+
+Keep `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` at line 65 intact — the new fields are opt-in with serde defaults.
+
+### 2. `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml`
+
+Annotate the six cross-cutting context-menu commands (lines 71-140):
+
+```yaml
+- id: entity.cut
+  context_menu_group: 1
+  context_menu_order: 0
+  # ...existing fields...
+- id: entity.copy
+  context_menu_group: 1
+  context_menu_order: 1
+- id: entity.paste
+  context_menu_group: 1
+  context_menu_order: 2
+- id: entity.delete
+  context_menu_group: 2
+  context_menu_order: 0
+- id: entity.archive
+  context_menu_group: 2
+  context_menu_order: 1
+- id: entity.unarchive
+  context_menu_group: 2
+  context_menu_order: 2
+```
+
+### 3. `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml`
+
+Annotate `ui.inspect` (lines 1-6):
+```yaml
+- id: ui.inspect
+  context_menu_group: 3
+  context_menu_order: 0
+  # ...existing fields...
+```
+
+### 4. `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs`
+
+Modify `emit_cross_cutting_commands` (lines 636-761):
+- Collect matched commands into a local `Vec<ResolvedCommand>` first instead of pushing straight into `result`.
+- Sort that local Vec by `(cmd_def.context_menu_group.unwrap_or(u32::MAX), cmd_def.context_menu_order.unwrap_or(0), cmd_def.id.clone())` — the id tiebreaker keeps same-group-same-order deterministic.
+- Set `ResolvedCommand.group` to `format!("{entity_type}:ctx{}", context_menu_group.unwrap_or(u32::MAX))` so distinct groups trigger separators in the frontend renderer.
+- Push into `result` via `push_dedup` in sorted order.
+- Preserve all existing trace logging (entry/filter/match/dedup spans).
+
+No changes to `emit_global_registry_commands`, `emit_scoped_registry_commands`, or dynamic emitters — they stay with their existing `group: "<category>"` strings and their menus don't need re-grouping.
+
+No changes to `context-menu.ts` frontend — its existing `lastGroup !== cmd.group` separator logic already does the right thing once the backend emits distinct group strings.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Right-clicking a task/tag/column/any entity shows commands in this order: `Cut`, `Copy`, `Paste`, `─── separator ───`, `Delete`, `Archive` (and `Unarchive` when available), `─── separator ───`, `Inspect <Entity>`.
+- [ ] Separators appear between the Cut/Copy/Paste block and the Delete/Archive block, and between the Delete/Archive block and Inspect.
+- [ ] Order is identical across process restarts (no HashMap-hash reshuffling).
+- [ ] The command palette is unaffected — palette entries are emitted via the same pipeline but the frontend palette renderer ignores `group`. No visible change there.
+- [ ] The native menu bar (Edit → Cut/Copy/Paste) is unaffected — `menu.group`/`menu.order` still drive that placement via `kanban-app/src/menu.rs::append_grouped_entries` (lines 192-212).
+- [ ] `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` on `CommandDef` still holds — the new optional fields are serde-defaultable so existing YAMLs without them parse unchanged.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New Rust test `cross_cutting_context_menu_is_ordered_and_grouped` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` tests module:
+  1. Build a minimal fields/registry/impls fixture with a single task moniker in scope chain.
+  2. Call `commands_for_scope(..., context_menu_only: true, ...)`.
+  3. Filter result to `entity.*` + `ui.inspect` entries.
+  4. Assert the order is exactly `[entity.cut, entity.copy, entity.paste, entity.delete, entity.archive, (entity.unarchive if emitted), ui.inspect]`.
+  5. Assert `cmds[0..3]` all have the same `group` string (contains `"ctx1"`), `cmds[3..5]` have the same group (contains `"ctx2"`), and the inspect entry has a third distinct group (contains `"ctx3"`).
+- [ ] New Rust test `cross_cutting_order_is_stable_across_runs` in the same test module:
+  1. Run `commands_for_scope` twice in the same test (fresh HashMap each time) and assert identical id sequences.
+  2. This guards against the current HashMap-iteration nondeterminism.
+- [ ] Update `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs` test `command_def_yaml_round_trip` (line 114-139) to exercise the new fields in at least one round-trip assertion.
+- [ ] Existing tests still pass:
+  - `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs` — `test_perspective_yaml_parses`, `keymap_commands_are_visible_in_palette`, merge tests
+  - `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_dispatch_integration.rs` — cross-cutting and menu-bar dedup tests
+  - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/context-menu.test.tsx` — renderer-side separator logic (unchanged, asserts behavior against the new group strings indirectly via test fixtures if any)
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-commands` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — all passing.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd`. Write `cross_cutting_context_menu_is_ordered_and_grouped` first — it should fail because today the order is arbitrary and all groups collapse to one string. Then add the two fields, annotate the YAMLs, and update the emitter.
+- Do not modify the frontend `context-menu.ts` renderer or the native menu builder (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs`). Both already do the right thing given the corrected backend emission.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value:
## What

Right-click context menus on any entity currently emit `entity.cut`, `entity.copy`, `entity.paste`, `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, `ui.inspect` as one undifferentiated block with **no separator and no stable order**. Two defects to fix at once:

1. **No grouping** — all cross-cutting commands get the same `ResolvedCommand.group` string in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs:748` (`group: entity_type.to_string()`). The frontend renderer at `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/context-menu.ts:53-69` inserts a separator only when `cmd.group !== lastGroup`, so with one group there are zero separators.
2. **No stable order** — `emit_cross_cutting_commands` (`scope_commands.rs:636-754`) iterates `all_registry_cmds` which is backed by a `HashMap<String, CommandDef>` (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs:24`). HashMap iteration is unordered and Rust's `DefaultHasher` reseeds per process, so the menu order differs run to run.

**Desired context-menu order** (user-specified + logical extension to cover all cross-cutting commands):
```
Cut <Entity>
Copy <Entity>
Paste <Entity>
──────────────
Delete <Entity>
Archive <Entity>
Unarchive <Entity>        (hidden by Command::available when N/A)
──────────────
Inspect <Entity>
```

## Approach

**Design choice**: Add two new optional CommandDef fields — `context_menu_group: Option<u32>` and `context_menu_order: Option<u32>` — and use them to drive sort + separator placement in the cross-cutting emitter. Chosen over reusing `menu.group`/`menu.order` because:
- `MenuPlacement` in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:43-57` couples grouping to native-menu-bar placement (requires a `path`). Cut/copy/paste are in the native Edit menu, but delete/archive/unarchive and inspect are not — and making them appear there silently is an unwanted side-effect.
- Keeps the two surfaces (native menu bar vs context menu) independently controllable.
- The field names state the intent explicitly at each call site.

### 1. `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs`

Add to `CommandDef` (inserted near `pub context_menu: bool` at line 84):

```rust
/// Priority bucket for context-menu placement. Commands with the same
/// `context_menu_group` render contiguously; a separator is inserted
/// between groups. Lower values render first. Omit for "uncategorised"
/// (sorts after all explicit groups).
#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
pub context_menu_group: Option<u32>,

/// Sort order within the same `context_menu_group`. Omit for default (0).
#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
pub context_menu_order: Option<u32>,
```

Keep `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` at line 65 intact — the new fields are opt-in with serde defaults.

### 2. `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml`

Annotate the six cross-cutting context-menu commands (lines 71-140):

```yaml
- id: entity.cut
  context_menu_group: 1
  context_menu_order: 0
  # ...existing fields...
- id: entity.copy
  context_menu_group: 1
  context_menu_order: 1
- id: entity.paste
  context_menu_group: 1
  context_menu_order: 2
- id: entity.delete
  context_menu_group: 2
  context_menu_order: 0
- id: entity.archive
  context_menu_group: 2
  context_menu_order: 1
- id: entity.unarchive
  context_menu_group: 2
  context_menu_order: 2
```

### 3. `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml`

Annotate `ui.inspect` (lines 1-6):
```yaml
- id: ui.inspect
  context_menu_group: 3
  context_menu_order: 0
  # ...existing fields...
```

### 4. `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs`

Modify `emit_cross_cutting_commands` (lines 636-761):
- Collect matched commands into a local `Vec<ResolvedCommand>` first instead of pushing straight into `result`.
- Sort that local Vec by `(cmd_def.context_menu_group.unwrap_or(u32::MAX), cmd_def.context_menu_order.unwrap_or(0), cmd_def.id.clone())` — the id tiebreaker keeps same-group-same-order deterministic.
- Set `ResolvedCommand.group` to `format!("{entity_type}:ctx{}", context_menu_group.unwrap_or(u32::MAX))` so distinct groups trigger separators in the frontend renderer.
- Push into `result` via `push_dedup` in sorted order.
- Preserve all existing trace logging (entry/filter/match/dedup spans).

No changes to `emit_global_registry_commands`, `emit_scoped_registry_commands`, or dynamic emitters — they stay with their existing `group: "<category>"` strings and their menus don't need re-grouping.

No changes to `context-menu.ts` frontend — its existing `lastGroup !== cmd.group` separator logic already does the right thing once the backend emits distinct group strings.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Right-clicking a task/tag/column/any entity shows commands in this order: `Cut`, `Copy`, `Paste`, `─── separator ───`, `Delete`, `Archive` (and `Unarchive` when available), `─── separator ───`, `Inspect <Entity>`.
- [ ] Separators appear between the Cut/Copy/Paste block and the Delete/Archive block, and between the Delete/Archive block and Inspect.
- [ ] Order is identical across process restarts (no HashMap-hash reshuffling).
- [ ] The command palette is unaffected — palette entries are emitted via the same pipeline but the frontend palette renderer ignores `group`. No visible change there.
- [ ] The native menu bar (Edit → Cut/Copy/Paste) is unaffected — `menu.group`/`menu.order` still drive that placement via `kanban-app/src/menu.rs::append_grouped_entries` (lines 192-212).
- [ ] `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` on `CommandDef` still holds — the new optional fields are serde-defaultable so existing YAMLs without them parse unchanged.

## Tests

- [ ] New Rust test `cross_cutting_context_menu_is_ordered_and_grouped` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` tests module:
  1. Build a minimal fields/registry/impls fixture with a single task moniker in scope chain.
  2. Call `commands_for_scope(..., context_menu_only: true, ...)`.
  3. Filter result to `entity.*` + `ui.inspect` entries.
  4. Assert the order is exactly `[entity.cut, entity.copy, entity.paste, entity.delete, entity.archive, (entity.unarchive if emitted), ui.inspect]`.
  5. Assert `cmds[0..3]` all have the same `group` string (contains `"ctx1"`), `cmds[3..5]` have the same group (contains `"ctx2"`), and the inspect entry has a third distinct group (contains `"ctx3"`).
- [ ] New Rust test `cross_cutting_order_is_stable_across_runs` in the same test module:
  1. Run `commands_for_scope` twice in the same test (fresh HashMap each time) and assert identical id sequences.
  2. This guards against the current HashMap-iteration nondeterminism.
- [ ] Update `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs` test `command_def_yaml_round_trip` (line 114-139) to exercise the new fields in at least one round-trip assertion.
- [ ] Existing tests still pass:
  - `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs` — `test_perspective_yaml_parses`, `keymap_commands_are_visible_in_palette`, merge tests
  - `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_dispatch_integration.rs` — cross-cutting and menu-bar dedup tests
  - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/context-menu.test.tsx` — renderer-side separator logic (unchanged, asserts behavior against the new group strings indirectly via test fixtures if any)
- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-commands` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — all passing.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd`. Write `cross_cutting_context_menu_is_ordered_and_grouped` first — it should fail because today the order is arbitrary and all groups collapse to one string. Then add the two fields, annotate the YAMLs, and update the emitter.
- Do not modify the frontend `context-menu.ts` renderer or the native menu builder (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs`). Both already do the right thing given the corrected backend emission.
Entity context menu: group Cut/Copy/Paste and Delete/Archive with stable ordering and separator

-1. **No grouping** — all cross-cutting commands get the same `ResolvedCommand.group` string in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs:748` (`group: entity_type.to_string()`). The frontend renderer at `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/context-menu.ts:53-69` inserts a separator only when `cmd.group !== lastGroup`, so with one group there are zero separators.

HashMap iteration is unordered and Rust's `DefaultHasher` reseeds per process, so the menu order differs run to run.

 ## Approach

-- `MenuPlacement` in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:43-57` couples grouping to native-menu-bar placement (requires a `path`). Cut/copy/paste are in the native Edit menu, but delete/archive/unarchive and inspect are not — and making them appear there silently is an unwanted side-effect.
